If you want a more polished *social/live* poker app rather than single-player, **EasyPoker** is good for real poker nights but it is designed for playing with friends, not solo play.[1]

- **Best single-player pick:** **Poker Offline** — best fit for offline/solo play on Android.[2]
- **Best for playing with friends:** **EasyPoker** — not single-player, but useful if you want real poker digitally.[1]

If you want, I can also give you:
- the **best free offline poker apps**
- the **best Indian real-money poker apps**
- or a **top 5 Android poker apps list** sorted by single-player quality

If you want an **Indian poker app that you can play without friends**, the best fit from the results is **Teen Patti: Indian Poker**, because it includes **online matches with real players** and also **offline mode** for practicing alone.[1]

Other good options are:

- **Poker Offline** — lets you play **offline against computer/bots** or **online with players from around the world** when you want to play without friends.[8]
- **EasyPoker** — best if you want **real poker without cards or chips** and can join by **PIN**, but it is mainly designed for **playing with friends** rather than solo play.[2][7]
- **Pokerrrr2** — good for **private poker games with friends**, but it is also centered on hosted friend tables, not solo play.[3]

If by “without friends” you mean **play alone against strangers or bots**, **Teen Patti: Indian Poker** and **Poker Offline** are the most relevant choices here.[1][8]

best offline card game Android free.response:

how to play Indian poker on Android.response:
If you want to **play Indian Poker on Android**, the simplest option is to install an app named **Indian poker** on Google Play; its listing says it lets you play Indian poker using playing cards and is intended for multiple people.[2] If you want a broader card-game app, **Teen Patti: Indian Poker** is another Android option that appears on Google Play and is described as an Indian poker/teen patti card game.[1]

**How Indian Poker is played** in the classic rules:
- Each player is dealt **one card face down** and then places it on their forehead **without looking** so everyone else can see it.[5][6][7]
- There is usually **one round of betting**.[5][7]
- After betting, everyone reveals their card and the **highest card wins**; some variants also rank suits, but the standard version usually does not.[5][7]
- The game is also known as **Blind Man’s Buff** in some rule sets.[5]

On Android, the gameplay in an app is typically:
- Start or join a room.
- Receive one card.
- Keep your card hidden from yourself but visible to others.
- Bet, fold, or continue depending on the app’s rules.
- Reveal at showdown to determine the winner.[2][5][7]

If you meant the **card game tutorial** rather than a specific app, the core strategy is to watch the other players’ visible cards and estimate whether your hidden card is likely to be high enough to stay in.[6]

Supports English/Hindi, challenging bot AI, and fully playable without internet.[[1]](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=india.poker.teenpatti.free&hl=en_US)

**Teen Patti Solo: 3 Patti Poker** – Focused single-player version of the classic Indian Teen Patti poker game for solo/offline play.[[2]](https://teen-patti-crore.en.softonic.com/android/alternatives)

These are available on the Google Play Store and emphasize single-player/offline modes against computer opponents, unlike most popular Teen Patti apps that are primarily multiplayer.

Indian poker app without friends.response:
**Ultimate Teen Patti** (by Games24x7) is a top recommendation. This popular Android app lets you play Teen Patti (Indian Poker) online with random strangers worldwide via public tables, without needing friends. It supports practice modes and has millions of users.[[1]](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.games24x7.teenpatti.playstore&hl=en_US)

**Octro Teen Patti** is another strong option. It offers real-time online multiplayer Teen Patti tables where you can join public games with other players, plus practice features. No friends required.[[2]](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.octro.teenpatti&hl=en_US)

For standard Texas Hold'em poker with random online opponents (playable solo-style), try **Poker Heat** or **Poker Offline**, both available on Google Play and supporting multiplayer against global players.[[3]](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.bigblueparrot.pokerfriends&hl=en_US)

These are free-to-download apps on the Google Play Store with in-app purchases or virtual chips. Search by exact name to install.

blind man's bluff card game app Android.response:
**Blindex — Blind Man's Bluff (Indian Poker)** is a free, offline single-player Android app available on the Google Play Store. It lets you play the card-on-forehead poker variant (Blind Man's Bluff/Indian Poker) head-to-head against an AI dealer, with options for different difficulty levels.[[1]](https://blindex.app/)

Search for "Blindex" directly in the Google Play Store to download it.

best offline card game Android free.response:

- **Solitaire - Offline Card Games** (by SNG): Classic Klondike/Patience Solitaire with customizable decks and backgrounds. Completely free, no internet required after download.[[1]](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.SNG.Solitaire.Free&hl=en_US)

- **Spades - Offline Card Games** (by SNG): High-quality classic Spades with AI opponents. Fully offline, ad-light, and free to play.[[2]](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.sngict.spades&hl=en_US)

- **Offline Card Games (free no wifi) feat. Gin Rummy**: Collection featuring Gin Rummy plus other classics like Hearts or Rummy variants. Designed for offline play without Wi-Fi.[[3]](https://download.cnet.com/offline-card-games-free-no-wifi-feat-gin-rummy/3000-2647_4-78380979.html)

- **Ultimate Offline Card Games**: Hub app with multiple titles including Rummy 500, Canasta, Solitaire, Spades, and Hearts—all playable offline.[[4]](https://ultimate-offline-card-games.en.uptodown.com/android)

These are popular, highly rated options focused on classic card games with strong offline support. Search for them directly on the Google Play Store.

how to play Indian poker on Android.response:
**Here are the best Android apps to play Indian Poker (including the classic forehead-card variant and the popular Teen Patti/Indian Poker version):**

- **Indian Poker** (by Kanbe)  
  Simple offline simulation app for playing Indian Poker with physical-style cards among multiple players. Great for casual, local multiplayer fun. Available on Google Play.[[1]](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.kanbe1365.indian_poker&hl=en_US)

- **Teen Patti: Indian Poker**  
  Digital version of the classic 3-card Indian Poker (Teen Patti) with betting, bonuses, and online play options. Fast-paced and beginner-friendly.[[2]](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=teen.patti.indian.poker&hl=en_NZ)

For real-money or competitive Indian-style poker, try **PokerBaazi** or **Junglee Poker** (both feature Teen Patti/Indian variants alongside other poker games). Download directly from the Google Play Store and follow the in-app tutorials to learn the rules while playing.
